perturbation.original_text: 13. Securities Laws . Each party is aware, and will advise its Representatives who are informed of the matters that are the subject of this Agreement, of the restrictions imposed by Federal and state securities laws on the purchase or sale of securities by it or its Representatives have received material, nonpublic information (i.e., information that is (i) specific, (ii) non- public and (iii) likely to have a material effect on the market price of the issuer’s securities if and when made public (such that a reasonable investor would consider the information material in deciding whether to buy, hold or sell the issuer’s securities)) from the issuer of such securities and on the communication of such information to any other person or entity when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person or entity is likely to purchase or sell securities.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Miscellaneous . Each party acknowledges and agrees that money damages may not be an adequate remedy for an actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, and the nonbreaching party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity. In case any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable, the validity, legality and enforceability of the remaining provisions of the Agreement shall not in any way be affected or impaired thereby and such provision will be deemed to be restated to reflect the original intention of the parties as nearly as possible in accordance with applicable law. Each party is aware, and will advise its Representatives who are informed of the matters that are the subject of this Agreement, of the restrictions imposed by Federal and state securities laws on the purchase or sale of securities by it or its Representatives have received material, nonpublic information (i.e., information that is (i) specific, (ii) non- public and (iii) likely to have a material effect on the market price of the issuer’s securities if and when made public (such that a reasonable investor would consider the information material in deciding whether to buy, hold or sell the issuer’s securities)) from the issuer of such securities and on the communication of such information to any other person or entity when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person or entity is likely to purchase or sell securities.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the Securities Laws disclosure to the 'Miscellaneous' section buries it where it might be overlooked. This diminishes its prominence and enforceability, as parties might not readily associate 'Miscellaneous' terms with critical insider trading restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Section 10(b) and Rule 10b-5
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 10b-5 prohibits the use of any manipulative or deceptive device in connection with the purchase or sale of securities. By obscuring the disclosure about securities laws within a miscellaneous section, the contract makes it more difficult to ensure that parties are aware of their obligations under these laws, potentially facilitating illegal insider trading activities. The placement undermines the clear communication of restrictions against using material non-public information, thus weakening compliance with the Securities Exchange Act.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. CFR prev | next § 240.10b-5 Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce, or of the mails or of any facility of any national securities exchange , (a) To employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud, (b) To make any untrue statement of a material fact or to omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements made, in the light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, or (c) To engage in any act, practice, or course of business which operates or would operate as a fraud or deceit upon any person, in connection with the purchase or sale of any security . (Sec. 10; 48 Stat. 891 ; 15 U.S.C. 78j ) [ 13 FR 8183 , Dec. 22, 1948, as amended at 16 FR 7928 , Aug. 11, 1951] CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts securities laws because it buries the disclosure about these laws within a 'Miscellaneous' section. The 'Perturbation Explanation' indicates this reduces the prominence and enforceability of the disclosure. The 'Law Explanation' states this placement 'undermines the clear communication of restrictions against using material non-public information, thus weakening compliance with the Securities Exchange Act'. According to 17 CFR § 240.10b-5, it is unlawful to 'employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud' or 'omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements made...not misleading'. By obscuring the disclosure, the contract makes it more difficult for parties to be aware of their obligations, potentially violating this rule. The 'Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction' perturbation definition applies, as a required legal disclosure is placed in a misleading section.

perturbation.original_text: 16. Governing Law; Forum . This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the internal laws of the State of New York, without giving effect to the principles of conflicts of laws thereof. In any dispute between the parties arising out of or relating to this Agreement, (i) each party irrevocably and unconditionally consents and submits to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the state and Federal courts located in the State of New York, (ii) if any action is commenced in state court, then, subject to applicable law, no party shall object to the removal of such action to any Federal court located in the State of New York, (iii) each party irrevocably waives the right to trial by jury and (iv) each party irrevocably consents to service of process by first class certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Non-Disclosure of Transaction . In addition, each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, such party and its Representatives will not disclose to any other person the existence of this Agreement, the fact that any Evaluation Material has been made available hereunder, that discussions are taking place concerning the Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto, or that the Transaction is being contemplated; provided, that a party may make such disclosure if, in the written opinion of such party’s outside counsel, such disclosure is necessary to avoid committing a violation of law. In such event, the party required to make such disclosure shall comply with the provisions of paragraph 5 below. This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the internal laws of the State of New York, without giving effect to the principles of conflicts of laws thereof. In any dispute between the parties arising out of or relating to this Agreement, (i) each party irrevocably and unconditionally consents and submits to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the state and Federal courts located in the State of New York, (ii) if any action is commenced in state court, then, subject to applicable law, no party shall object to the removal of such action to any Federal court located in the State of New York, (iii) each party irrevocably waives the right to trial by jury and (iv) each party irrevocably consents to service of process by first class certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the governing law and forum selection clause into the section on 'Non-Disclosure of Transaction' could make it harder to find and enforce. Normally, these clauses are located near the end of the contract within the 'Miscellaneous' or 'General Provisions' section. Placing it here implies it only applies to disputes related to non-disclosure, potentially creating ambiguity about which jurisdiction governs other aspects of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: New York Civil Practice Law and Rules (CPLR) § 301
perturbation.law_explanation: NY CPLR § 301 establishes the basis for personal jurisdiction over persons, partnerships, or corporations in New York State. By placing the governing law and forum selection clause under 'Non-Disclosure,' a party could argue that the consent to jurisdiction only applies to non-disclosure matters and not to other disputes arising from the contract, creating confusion and potentially requiring separate jurisdictional analyses for different claims.

Jurisdiction over persons, property or status. A court may exercise such jurisdiction over persons, property, or status as might have been exercised heretofore.
perturbation.justification: The 'Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction' perturbation type is applicable here because the governing law clause, which typically appears in a general provisions section, has been moved to the 'Non-Disclosure of Transaction' section. This relocation creates a structural flaw that could lead to ambiguity and potential disputes regarding the scope of the governing law and forum selection clause. As the Law Explanation points out, a party could argue that the consent to jurisdiction only applies to non-disclosure matters. This contradicts the standard legal practice of having a clear, broadly applicable governing law clause and makes it difficult to enforce under state law. Scraped Law Snippet 1, NY CPLR § 301, touches on jurisdiction over persons, property, or status. While it doesn't explicitly state where a governing law clause should be placed, the implication is that jurisdiction should be clear and easily ascertainable. Moving the clause introduces ambiguity, which undermines this principle by requiring additional legal interpretation to determine its scope.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Non-Solicitation . In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished to you, each party agrees that for a period of one year from the date set forth above, without the other party’s prior written consent, neither party nor its affiliates will solicit, directly or indirectly, to employ any person who is now employed by the other party or its affiliates and is directly involved in the discussions hereunder (a “Covered Person”). The term “solicit to employ” shall not be deemed to include: (i) general solicitations of employment not specifically directed toward employees of the other party, (ii) an event in which such Covered Person contacts the such party or its Representatives on the Covered Person’s own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such party or its Representatives, or (iii) such Covered Person is referred to such party by search firms, employment agencies, or other similar entities, provided that such entities have not been specifically instructed by such party to solicit the employees of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. No Representation of Accuracy . Each party understands and acknowledges that neither party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material made available by it or to it. Each party agrees that neither party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of or reliance upon such other party’s Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations or warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the Transaction, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect. In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished to you, each party agrees that for a period of one year from the date set forth above, without the other party’s prior written consent, neither party nor its affiliates will solicit, directly or indirectly, to employ any person who is now employed by the other party or its affiliates and is directly involved in the discussions hereunder (a “Covered Person”). The term “solicit to employ” shall not be deemed to include: (i) general solicitations of employment not specifically directed toward employees of the other party, (ii) an event in which such Covered Person contacts the such party or its Representatives on the Covered Person’s own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such party or its Representatives, or (iii) such Covered Person is referred to such party by search firms, employment agencies, or other similar entities, provided that such entities have not been specifically instructed by such party to solicit the employees of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By relocating the non-solicitation clause to the section addressing 'No Representation of Accuracy', the contractual provision becomes less prominent. Non-solicitation agreements are often viewed as separate covenants within a contract. Placing this clause under 'No Representation of Accuracy' implies that the non-solicitation agreement is related to the accuracy of the Evaluation Material, when it's really intended to address hiring practices.
perturbation.law_explanation: Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 203 dictates that 'express terms are given greater weight than course of performance, course of dealing, and usage of trade.' A court might interpret the non-solicitation clause as less important or directly tied to the representation of accuracy of the material disclosed, due to its placement. This interpretation could undermine the enforceability of the non-solicitation agreement, especially if a party argues the breach was not directly linked to inaccuracies in the shared information.
perturbation.justification: The modification falls under the category of 'Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction'. The relocation of the non-solicitation clause to the section addressing 'No Representation of Accuracy' weakens the enforceability of the agreement. While the clause itself is still present, its placement implies that the non-solicitation agreement is related to the accuracy of the Evaluation Material, which is not its intended purpose. The LLM's 'Law Explanation' correctly states that Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 203 dictates that 'express terms are given greater weight than course of performance, course of dealing, and usage of trade.' By placing the non-solicitation clause under a less relevant heading, the contract structurally undermines its importance, making it potentially more difficult to enforce. The absence of scraped snippets does not negate this assessment, as the reasoning relies on the principle of contract interpretation regarding the prominence of terms.
